name: ION-14382 Booking DynamoDB Review Follow-up - Map vs String, Tests, UUID, Streaming

entries.summary: Comprehensive analysis of 4 user concerns: (1) Map vs String serialization - NO constraint in SDK v2 prevents Map storage, registration module already does it; (2) Test counts: 913 unit tests (TestNG: 911+ JUnit5), 149 integration tests (9 DynamoDB IT classes); (3) UUID generation handled in services for all models; (4) @DynamoDBStream(KEYS_ONLY) was removed from BookingDetail but annotation exists in cloud-sdk-aws
entries.detail:
Key findings:
1. Map vs String: EnrichedAttributes converter currently serializes to String(S) but can use Map(M). LegacyMapConverter already handles read from Map. Need reverse converter for write. Registration module's AuditConverter proves Map(M) works.
2. Tests: 199 JUnit-only files, 79 TestNG-only files, 23 both. Unit total ~913 (incl 9 skipped). Integration: 149 tests in 9 IT classes - all DynamoDB DAO ITs.
3. UUIDs: BookingDetail uses calcBookingId() UUID, SpotRates uses calcRandomUUID(), others use business keys. No auto-generation lost.
4. Streaming: @DynamoDBStream(StreamViewType.KEYS_ONLY) from com.inttra.mercury.cloudsdk.database.annotation.DynamoDBStream must be re-added to BookingDetail.

entries.summary: All 1683 unit tests + 149 integration tests PASS after converting converters from String(S) to native Map(M)/List(L) type and restoring @DynamoDBStream(KEYS_ONLY) on BookingDetail
entries.detail:
After converting all 5 converters from String(S) to Map(M) type and adding @DynamoDBStream(KEYS_ONLY) back to BookingDetail:
- Unit tests: 1683 run, 0 failures (1 skipped)
- Integration tests: 149 run, 0 failures
- All DynamoDB DAO integration tests pass with Map format

Converters changed: AuditAttributeConverter, EnrichedAttributesConverter, MetaDataConverter, ConditionListAttributeConverter (to L type), RangeAttributeConverter
All converters now write native DynamoDB Map/List types matching production format.
Backward compat maintained: converters still read JSON String(S) format as fallback.

entries.summary: Session 3 continuation: Fixed IT test failure, added streaming IT test, added production-realistic EnrichedAttributes IT test. All 287 IT tests pass (0 failures). Model: Claude Opus 4.6
entries.detail:
Changes made in this session continuation:

1. FIXED: BookingDetailDaoIT production-realistic test failure - TransactionParty.partyScac is @JsonIgnore so not persisted in enrichedAttributes Map. Removed assertion for partyScac.

2. ADDED: Streaming annotation IT test in BookingDynamoDbAdminCommandIT - verifies table has streamEnabled=true with KEYS_ONLY view type after admin command creates tables.

3. ADDED: Production-realistic EnrichedAttributes round-trip IT test - uses complex nested data matching production enrichedAttributes.json: 4 LocationAdditionalInfo with Country (with identifiers) + Geography, 3 TransactionParty with Address, ContainerType, MultiVersionAttributes with partyMap.

Key finding: TransactionParty.partyScac has @JsonIgnore — it's an enrichment-time field set by business logic, not persisted in DynamoDB enrichedAttributes Map. This is correct behavior.

Test results after fixes:
- Failsafe: 287 completed, 0 failures, 0 errors
- Surefire (unit): 914 run, 0 failures, 10 skipped
- All 9 DynamoDB IT classes pass

Files changed:
- BookingDetailDaoIT.java: Added production-realistic EA test, fixed partyScac assertion
- BookingDynamoDbAdminCommandIT.java: Added streaming annotation verification test

entries.summary: Completed full update of aws-dynamo-review.md — all sections now reflect Map(M) native format
entries.detail:
Document update completed across all sections:
- Executive summary: Updated test counts (914 unit/287 IT), added streaming annotation row, renamed converter row
- Section 3.1 BookingDetail: audit/enrichedAttributes/metaData → Map(M), streaming → restored
- Section 3.2 SpotRatesDetail: audit → Map(M) 
- Section 3.3 SpotRatesToInttraRef: audit → Map(M)
- Section 3.4 RapidReservation: audit/conditionList/range → Map(M)/List(L)
- Section 4.1: Converter table already updated (prev session) to show Map(M) native format
- Section 4.2: Already rewritten (prev session) as "Native Map Storage" with design rationale
- Section 7: Rewritten — 7.1 Converter Storage Format, 7.2 GSI Projection, 7.3 Streaming Annotation Restored, 7.4 New Tests (including IT tests)
- Section 8: Updated counts — unit 914/10skipped, IT 287 (149 TestNG + 138 JUnit5), added test framework breakdown table and mvn commands
- Section 9: Risk table updated — Map format is now "None" severity, added Streams row
- Section 10 (NEW): UUID/Primary Key Generation Verification — all 7 models verified, none lost auto-generation
Agent: GitHub Copilot using Claude Opus 4.6 model

entries.summary: Created PR review response document addressing all 17 review comments
entries.detail:
Created PR review response document at .github/skills/pr-reviews/pr-review-responses.md. Addressed all 17 review comments:
- 15 were queries about why annotations were removed — explained SDK v1 → v2 migration pattern (DynamoDBDocument removal, DynamoDBIgnore removal, converter pattern, entity annotation changes)
- 1 was a real catch (DynamoDBStream annotation missing) — already fixed and tested
- 1 was a concern about CreateTables.java deletion — explained replacement with DynamoDbAdminCommand and cross-module precedent
Key architectural points documented:
1. @DynamoDBDocument (80 removals) not needed because converters use Jackson ObjectMapper
2. @DynamoDBIgnore (139 removals) not needed because nested classes aren't scanned by Enhanced Client
3. DTOs (ConfirmPendingMessage, BookingRequestContract, CancelMessage) are NOT DynamoDB entities — stored via ContractAttributeConverter
4. CreateTables replaced by BookingDynamoDbAdminCommand with annotation-driven table creation
5. All converters replaced with SDK v2 AttributeConverter implementations
